academic_requirements.summary: The programme has two study tracks. For foreign-title applicants, the official application pages require a minimum three-year bachelor's degree or equivalent in Engineering, Physical Sciences and Technologies, Computer Science and Technology, or Mathematical Sciences, with deep knowledge of Mathematics, Physics, and Statistics. The 2026/27 official admission call also distinguishes an open-access Mathematical Modelling for Engineering and Science track and a limited-access Financial Engineering track [uncertain].
academic_requirements.prior_degree_or_diploma: Bachelor diploma (or equivalent), with a minimum three-year undergraduate degree in Engineering, Physical Sciences and Technologies, Computer Science and Technology, or Mathematical Sciences.
academic_requirements.subject_requirements: Applicants must have deep knowledge in Mathematics, Physics, and Statistics. For the Italian-title 2026/27 call, the university also evaluates curricular requirements and personal preparation by study track [uncertain].
academic_requirements.gpa_or_country_requirements: Foreign-title applicants must verify country-based specific entry title and GPA requirements on the University of Padua application platform; no single universal GPA threshold is published on the foreign-applicant pages. The 2026/27 Italian-title call uses track-specific academic evaluation and ranking rules for Financial Engineering [uncertain].

accepted_proofs: TOEFL iBT with a minimum score of 80.
IELTS Academic or General Training with a minimum score of 6.0.
Cambridge English B2 First with a minimum score of 173.
Trinity College London Integrated Skills in English ISE II.
Oxford Test of English with a minimum score of 126.
Pearson English International Certificate Level 3 Upper Intermediate.
Pearson Test of English Academic with a minimum score of 65.
exemption_rules: Applicants are exempt if their previous education leading to the entry qualification was completed entirely in English.
Applicants are exempt if they are native English speakers from one of the countries recognized by the University of Padua.
language_requirements.deadline: 31 July 2026 for the limited-access Financial Engineering track and 31 August 2026 for the open-access Mathematical Modelling for Engineering and Science track.

application_deadline_eu.call_one: Mathematical Modelling for Engineering and Science: 2 November 2025 - 2 February 2026.
application_deadline_eu.call_two: Mathematical Modelling for Engineering and Science: 2 March 2026 - 2 May 2026. Financial Engineering: 15 July 2026 - 31 August 2026 for pre-enrolment and minimum-requirements application [uncertain].
application_deadline_eu.call_three: Mathematical Modelling for Engineering and Science: 2 June 2026 - 2 August 2026 (only for EU students and non-EU students residing in Italy).
application_deadline_eu.notes: The programme has two official admission routes. The Mathematical Modelling for Engineering and Science track is open access on apply.unipd.it. The Financial Engineering track is limited access and the 2026/27 call for admission sets a separate summer procedure and ranking for EU or equivalent candidates.

application_deadline_non_eu.call_one: Mathematical Modelling for Engineering and Science: 2 November 2025 - 2 February 2026.
application_deadline_non_eu.call_two: Mathematical Modelling for Engineering and Science: 2 March 2026 - 2 May 2026. Financial Engineering: 7 January 2026 - 7 March 2026 for non-EU applicants residing outside Italy.
application_deadline_non_eu.call_three: Not applicable for non-EU applicants residing outside Italy for the Mathematical Modelling track; the third call is reserved for EU students and non-EU students residing in Italy.
application_deadline_non_eu.notes: Financial Engineering is a limited-access track with 3 places for non-EU candidates residing outside Italy. The open-access Mathematical Modelling track follows the general open-access international calls.

required_documents.notes: If needed, an English or Italian translation must be uploaded at application stage. No legalized documents are required at application stage.

required_documents.document_name: Academic qualification or provisional enrollment certificate
required_documents.notes: If the final qualification has not yet been awarded, an enrollment or provisional certificate is accepted.
EU applicants and non-EU applicants residing in Italy may use an ID card; other applicants normally provide a passport.
Required only for non-EU citizens residing in Italy.

required_documents.document_name: English language certificate or accepted exemption proof
required_documents.notes: Required unless the applicant qualifies for an official exemption.

required_documents.document_name: Bachelor's diploma translated into Italian or English and legalized
required_documents.notes: A post-secondary qualification awarded by a non-university higher education institution is also accepted if it allows admission to master's degrees.

required_documents.document_name: Transcript of Records translated into Italian or English and legalized
required_documents.notes: The transcript should state passed exams, grades, and preferably ECTS credits and programme description.

required_documents.document_name: Universitaly pre-enrolment and Study Visa D - enrolment
required_documents.notes: Non-EU citizens residing outside Italy must pre-enrol through Universitaly no later than 15 July and need a study visa to enter Italy.

required_documents.document_name: Residence permit or residence-permit application receipt (Assicurata)
required_documents.notes: Required for non-EU applicants after arrival in Italy; the residence-permit application must be filed within 8 working days.

entry_exam_or_test.test_name: No written entry test for Mathematical Modelling for Engineering and Science; ranking-based selection for Financial Engineering
entry_exam_or_test.applies_to: Financial Engineering applicants must place high enough in the ranking list; the Mathematical Modelling for Engineering and Science track has no ranking list.
entry_exam_or_test.dates: Financial Engineering pre-enrolment and minimum-requirements application run from 15 July 2026 to 31 August 2026 for the 2026/27 call; the ranking list is part of the limited-access procedure.
entry_exam_or_test.scoring_or_threshold: For Financial Engineering, the ranking is formulated on the weighted average of exam marks, alternatively the number of exams graded 30/30 or equivalent, and in case of a further tie priority goes to the younger candidate.

source_quotes.quote: deep knowledge in Mathematics, Physics and Statistics
This is a Limited access programme.
The ranking list is formulated on the basis of the following criteria
31st July for limited access degree programmes; 31st August for open access degree programmes.

uncertainty_notes: The programme-level page labels Mathematical Engineering as open access, but the official 2026/27 sources split it into an open-access Mathematical Modelling for Engineering and Science track and a limited-access Financial Engineering track.

For foreign-title applicants, the application platform gives field-of-study and country-based GPA rules, while the detailed 2026/27 call for admission published in English is the Italian-title call; programme-level normalization therefore mixes both official routes.
The EU timeline for the Financial Engineering track is reconstructed from the 2026/27 official call because the foreign-title application page still shows the EU call as TBD.
